final_response:
Flagged. This is the right shape for `observations.md` — a recurring, cross-project pattern that would cost time to re-derive next time we onboard an external contractor. It's not a commitment (so not `decisions.md`), and it spans projects (so not project-scoped). I logged it two places:

1. **`observations.md`** — new entry `2026-05-17 — IT credential provisioning lag for external contractors`. Three instances (May, March, February), ~4-day lag each time. Stated as a rule: file the IT request before the engagement starts, and plan the first week around tasks that don't need access.
2. **`daybook/2026-05-17.md`** — Log line pointing to the observation, so the daybook chronology reflects how the entry got there.

If this escalates into a commitment (e.g., "we will not start a contractor before IT confirms access"), that would graduate to `decisions.md` with the observation cited as Context. For now it's a pattern worth surfacing, not a constraint we've signed up to.

actions.content_summary: New daybook entry for today with Log line recording the contractor-onboarding pattern and noting promotion to observations.md.

Appended observation '2026-05-17 — IT credential provisioning lag for external contractors' (Generated-by: user). Three instances cited, ~4-day lag, with a named rule for scoping external contractor starts.

notes: Routed to observations.md rather than decisions.md: the user surfaced a recurring pattern and asked not to lose track, not a commitment that constrains future action. If it later crystallizes into a commitment, the methodology says to promote it to decisions.md citing this observation.
Not project-scoped — pattern spans every external contractor onboarding, so a single projects/<name>/ folder would have been the wrong home.
Set Generated-by: user since the user originated the finding; the agent only routed it.
Created today's daybook (none existed) and logged the routing action there so the chronology reflects how the observation got promoted.
Left Intent and Handoff sections of the daybook empty — session just started; user can fill Intent when the day's purpose firms up, and Handoff at close.
Did not run close-session — only the user signals end of session.
